# 054. Fake negative reviews and competitor sabotage

> **Use boundary:** This file is written for research, ethics, detection, and policy analysis. It describes mechanisms at a conceptual level so they can be studied, criticised, or detected. It is not an execution guide for manipulating platforms, audiences, investors, advertisers, or creators.


## Signal layer

**Credibility**

## Primary domain

Marketplaces / local search

## What the tactic tries to make outsiders believe

A competitor has quality or trust problems.

## Conceptual methodology

False negative reviews, reports, or claims are used to damage a competitor’s rating or platform access.

This is not a technical recipe. The research value is in understanding which proxy signal is being distorted and what kind of false inference the audience, platform, advertiser, or investor may draw.

## Signal corrupted

The tactic corrupts evidence that would normally help outsiders estimate real demand, trust, relevance, influence, or causality.

## Ethical problem

The tactic becomes ethically problematic when it hides material context, fabricates or inflates evidence, misattributes causality, or causes outsiders to reward an appearance of demand that does not match the underlying reality.

## Research red flags

Review bursts from non-customers; repeated complaint pattern; competitor timing; sudden rating drop.

## Safer ethical alternative

Use transparent paid promotion, clear disclosure, honest metrics, auditable attribution, and real customer evidence. Improve the underlying value rather than falsifying the evidence of value.

## Evidence strength

Depends on the domain. Treat platform policies and enforcement actions as strong evidence for rules; treat community chatter as weak evidence for operator culture only.

## Source notes

- **FTC final rule banning fake reviews and testimonials (2024)** (Regulator / primary). Prohibits fake/false reviews and testimonials, buying reviews conditioned on sentiment, review suppression, insider reviews without disclosure, and misuse of fake social-media indicators. URL: https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2024/08/federal-trade-commission-announces-final-rule-banning-fake-reviews-testimonials
- **Amazon continues legal actions to protect customers from fake reviews** (Company enforcement / primary). Amazon reported legal actions against fake review brokers and said nearly 350,000 people using broker websites were no longer incentivized to write fake/misleading Amazon reviews. URL: https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/policy-news-views/amazon-continues-legal-actions-to-protect-customers-from-fake-reviews
- **Google Business Profile / Maps prohibited content — fake engagement** (Platform policy / primary). Google Maps contributions must reflect genuine experience; fake engagement includes incentivized reviews, multiple accounts to manipulate rating, and emulator/device-tampering methods. URL: https://support.google.com/business/answer/2622994?hl=en
